---
title: 'A solution to being irritated and mad at God, from Psalms 4'
date: '2015-08-19T06:39:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3950759948585685650'
blogger_filename: '/2015/08/a-solution-to-being-irritated-and-mad.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<i><br /></i><i><br /></i><i><br /></i><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>*This post is a little closer to home than usual... but I promised transparency. I hope you get something out of it. This came from my meditations this evening on Psalms 4.</i></blockquote><br /><br /><br /><h3>Lately... Oh the past 30-35 years... I've been irritated at God.&nbsp;</h3><br /><h4>Why you ask? Being born.&nbsp;</h4><br />It sounds odd at first, coming from someone who is pursing <a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/got-freedom.html" target="_blank">LIFE</a>. But my bent, my default if you will, during times of idleness or pause, is to be mad at God. Ever since my earliest days as a child I wanted to leave earth for my home in heaven. I could not understand why we must stay on this rotten planet.<br /><br />I often find myself remembering a scene from one of my favorite movies:<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><a href="http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0108358/quotes?item=qt0428063" target="_blank"><b>Tombstone (1993) IMDB:</b></a></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: #fcfae7; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 15px; line-height: 21px; margin-bottom: 0.3em; padding: 0px;"><span class="character" style="font-weight: bold;">Wyatt Earp</span>: What makes a man like Ringo, Doc? ..makes him do the things he does?<br /><span class="character" style="font-weight: bold;">Doc Holliday</span>: A man like Ringo has got a great empty hole, right through the middle of him. He can never kill enough, or steal enough, or inflict enough pain to ever fill it.<br /><span class="character" style="font-weight: bold;">Wyatt Earp</span>: What does he need?<br /><span class="character" style="font-weight: bold;">Doc Holliday</span>: Revenge.<br /><span class="character" style="font-weight: bold;">Wyatt Earp</span>: For what?<br /><span class="character" style="font-weight: bold;">Doc Holliday</span>: Bein' born.</blockquote><br /><ul><li><a href="https://youtu.be/Joi2F60Veqo" target="_blank">(YouTube Link here)&nbsp;</a></li></ul><br /><br />I feel like Doc Holliday sometimes, in that I know Ringo well because a part of me (<i>however small</i>) is Ringo. I've simply chosen to put that "old man" to death in Jesus. Nevertheless, the old man whispers to me from time to time, especially when I'm tired and worn out.<br /><br />I may not have killed anyone, or stolen anything... but I've inflicted pain. We all have, as sinners in a broken world. We inflict pain on the ones closest to us, as we operate out of self-defensive postures and broken sinful natures.<br /><br />My past (especially the one from before I rededicated my life to Jesus in 2002) is riddled with broken hearts, broken promises, and burned bridges.<br /><br />Without Jesus... I'm just a less violent Ringo...<br /><br />We all, as sinners cause pain in the life's of others.<br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b><i>Hurt people, hurt people.&nbsp;</i></b></blockquote><br />But even though I put that old man to death, and I live a new life in Christ Jesus, I still find myself angry at God.<br /><br />Why?<br /><br />For being born.<br /><br />Why?<br /><br />For the record: No... I'm not suicidal.<br /><br />I'm not looking for ways cause my own death. I'm just irritated that I have to stay. Kind of wish He'd come back already and take us home!<br /><br />This life is TOUGH sometimes...<br /><br /><ul><li>There are my own emotions to process.&nbsp;</li><li>Other peoples emotions to process.&nbsp;</li><li>The older you get the more responsibilities you get. There's a mortgage, car note, lawn to mow...&nbsp;</li><li>...and just when you are caught up, something else breaks or needs your attention.&nbsp;</li></ul><br /><br />Frankly... I'm not finding a lot to stay here for most days. I felt that way as a young child, and never stopped feeling that way. There are moments, seasons, where I am so engaged in one thing or another that I forget... but in idle moments it comes back.<br /><br />Why should I have to stay here, when I know there is something better on the other side? Why must I put up with this crazy stupid world?<br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><br /></i><i><b>*(Notice the amount of focus on&nbsp;</b></i><i><b>"Me",&nbsp;</b></i><i><b>"Myself", and&nbsp;</b></i><i><b>"I" in those statements, we'll address that in a moment.)</b></i></blockquote><br />It's one thing for someone to do something that makes you feel angry. That's easy enough to forgive and move on from.<br /><br />But what about if you are angry at God?<br /><br /><h4>Which brings me to Psalms 4: The answer to being angry at God</h4><br /><h4><a href="http://biblehub.com/esv/psalms/4.htm" target="_blank">Psalms 4</a></h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>1Answer me when I call, O God of&nbsp;<b>my righteousness</b>!<b>&nbsp;You have given me relief when I was in distress</b>. Be gracious to me and hear my prayer! 2O men,a how long shall my honor be turned into shame? How long will you love vain words and seek after lies?</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>Selah</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>3<b>But know that the LORD has set apart the godly for himself; the LORD hears when I call to him.</b></i><b>&nbsp;</b></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>4<b>Be angry, and do not sin; ponder in your own hearts on your beds, and be silent.</b></i><b>&nbsp;</b></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>Selah</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b><i>5Offer right sacrifices, and put your trust in the LORD.</i>&nbsp;</b></blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i>6There are many who say, â€œWho will show us some good? Lift up the light of your face upon us, O LORD!â€</i>&nbsp;</blockquote><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><b>7You have put more joy in my heart</b>&nbsp;than they have when their grain and wine abound. 8<b>In peace I will both lie down and sleep</b>; for you alone, O LORD, make me dwell in safety.</i></blockquote><div><br /></div><br /><h4>As I've been pondering Psalms 4 tonight, here are my take aways.</h4><br /><ul><li><b>Jesus is my Righteousness.&nbsp;</b></li></ul><ul><ul><li>He has delivered me from times of confusion and distress countless times, more often than I even recognize.&nbsp;</li><li>It's when I forget this, that I allow the enemy to confuse my mind and emotions.</li></ul></ul><ul><li><b>I was set apart through Grace, and through Grace He hears me. Every time.&nbsp;</b></li></ul><ul><ul><li>There is no room for doubt on this point.&nbsp;</li><li>I can never feel as though my prayers hit the wall or ceiling and fell deaf.&nbsp;</li><li><b>My Father ALWAYS hears me!!!!!!!</b></li></ul></ul><br /><ul><li><b>I was set apart for His purposes.&nbsp;</b></li></ul><ul><ul><li><b>Depression only works when you are focused on self.&nbsp;</b><b>Hence the "I" statements earlier.</b></li><li>The moment you realize, accept, AND engage with the idea that God has a purpose and a plan for your life, depression cannot operate.&nbsp;</li><li>The more deeply you are focused on others, the less you have time, room, or mental real estate for self.</li><li>In reverse, the more focused you are on self, the less you are spending your mental real estate on others.</li><li>"Others" and "Self" are mutually exclusive propositions.</li></ul></ul><br /><ul><li><b>HOW can I be angry without sinning? How can I be angry at God, without sinning?</b></li></ul><br /><ul><ul><li><b>First: He knows. He can handle it.</b></li><ul><li>He's God... kind of in the job description.</li><li>He's LOVE personified. He doesn't care that you are angry at Him. He wants to talk to you about it so you can move past it.</li><li>You're not God. You can't fix what is broken inside of you. You can't make it right inside of you. Only he can.&nbsp;</li><li>Like in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twelve-step_program#Twelve_Steps" target="_blank">A.A</a>., <b>admit you are powerless to change</b>, and that will begin the process of letting Him enact the change.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul></ul><blockquote class="tr_bq"><i><b>You can and must participate in the change, but you cannot change yourself.&nbsp;</b></i></blockquote><br /><ul><ul><li><b>Next: Tell Him.</b></li><ul><li>There's no point in trying to ignore the feeling, or pretend it's not there.&nbsp;</li><li>He knows.&nbsp;</li><li>Get is out, let him know how you feel.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul></ul><ul><ul><li><b>Ask: What lie am I believing right now?</b></li><ul><li><b>Any feeling that didn't come from Him is a result of a lie (however subconscious) you are believing.&nbsp;</b></li><li>Click (<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/05/stronghold-cycles-why-do-i-keep-making.html" target="_blank">here</a>) for a refresher on <a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/05/stronghold-cycles-why-do-i-keep-making.html" target="_blank">Strong Hold Cycles</a>, and lies.</li></ul></ul></ul><br /><ul><li><b>Be Still - Meditate</b></li></ul><ul><ul><li>Once you admit to God you've been angry at him, and you've asked him about the lies...&nbsp;</li><li><b>Be Still and Meditate.</b></li><li>Meditate means to speak to yourself softly, repeat, murmur, pray... continuing to keep your focus on Him.&nbsp;</li><li>It's more about listening and receiving, than speaking and putting information out. Most modern prayer consists of the prayer doing 90-100% of the talking. True biblical meditation would be the opposite. Letting GOD do 90-100% of the talking, you just be still, listen for his voice.&nbsp;</li><li>He may speak words, phrases, sentences... or he may speak in dreams, images, visions, ideas, seeing one thing and another, then seeing how they connect.</li><li>Just as a Zip File is a quick download that must be unpacked... the meditation downloads from HolySpirit often come quickly, but need time to unpack in the hearer.&nbsp;</li><li><b>This is the key to being angry and sinning not... Let him work it out.&nbsp;</b></li></ul></ul><ul><li><b>HOW can I learn to trust God?&nbsp;</b></li></ul><ul><ul><li>Same way... the result of this type of being still, and meditating is a developing trust in God.&nbsp;</li><li>Once you begin to hear, you choose to trust it. As your hearing turns into instructions, you choose to obey it.&nbsp;</li><li>As your hearing gets clearer, and your obeying gets more intuitive... the result is a trust in God.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul><ul><li><b>Result: Joy and Peace</b></li></ul><ul><ul><li>This process will lead to Joy and Peace.</li><li>Conversely, if you are lacking Joy and/or Peace, this is a sure sign you haven't been spending time Being Still and Meditating.&nbsp;</li><li><b>Red Light: Let this be a dashboard indicator for your life.</b></li><li><b>Lack of Joy/Peace = Lack of Meditation/Being Still</b></li></ul></ul><ul><li><b>Warning: You will experience resistance.&nbsp;</b></li></ul><ul><ul><li><b>There is a strong probability that you will start this meditation and get discouraged because you don't hear right away... or distracted so you get up to write a blog post...</b></li><li>This is because you have a real enemy who hates you, and doesn't want you to get close to God.</li><li><b>If at first you don't succeed... try, try, again.</b></li><li>This is about process, not destinations or check lists.</li><li>You don't develop a friendship, work collaboration, or romantic relationship in a single meeting. You might have the seed of one planted during a mere five minute meeting, but it will not blossom and grow without continual meetings to follow.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b><i style="background-color: cyan;">So it is with God... You will gain more from a five minute meeting every day, than a 35 minute meeting once a week.&nbsp;</i></b></blockquote><br /><div><h4>Your turn to share:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b><br /></b><b>What did you hear from God today, while reading Psalms 4 and/or this post?</b></blockquote><b><br /></b><b>Comment on this post, or Hit me up on Twitter&nbsp;<a href="https://twitter.com/darrellwolfe" target="_blank">@DarrellWolfe&nbsp;</a>,&nbsp;Google&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank">+DarrellWolfe</a>, Facebook&nbsp;<a href="https://www.facebook.com/DarrellGWolfe" target="_blank">DarrellGWolfe</a>, Etc...</b></div><b><br /></b><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>Tweet These:</b><br />#<a href="http://ctt.ec/8E5X7" target="_blank">TellYourStory</a>#<a href="http://ctt.ec/5xc67" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a></blockquote><br /><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><br /><div><ul><li><span style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">New Here? Start HERE</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/got-freedom.html" target="_blank">Got LIFE?</a></span></li><li><a href="http://eepurl.com/MzAVr" target="_blank">Insight In Your Inbox: Sign up for email delivery here</a>.&nbsp;</li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/banking-basics-for-believers-book-by.html" target="_blank">Click Here To Get My Book: "Banking Basics for Believers"</a>, which guides you through the basics of banking and money from a Christian Perspective.&nbsp;</li></ul></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="232" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" width="320" /></a></div><div><br /><br /><b>Other Hobby Sties by Darrell:</b><br /><br /><ul><li><a href="http://discerningthetime.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">http://discerningthetime.blogspot.com</a></li><ul><li>End Times Prophecy Study</li></ul><li><a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/" target="_blank">http://authorfun.blogspot.com</a></li><ul><li>Fiction and Flash Fiction Practice</li></ul><li><a href="http://www.lifeinfortworth.com/" target="_blank">http://www.lifeinfortworth.com</a></li><ul><li>Discover, Navigate, and Enjoy Fort Worth Texas. Reviews</li></ul></ul><div><br /></div></div>
